title: ChatGPT launched as a public research preview
summary: OpenAI introduced ChatGPT in late 2022 as a research preview focused on conversational interaction and user feedback.
fact_1: It was released publicly in November 2022.
fact_2: It helped popularize chatbot-style access to large language models.
fact_3: OpenAI framed the launch as a way to learn from real-world use.

title: GPT-4 was released as a multimodal model
summary: OpenAI released GPT-4 in 2023 as a large multimodal model with stronger capabilities and alignment than earlier GPT systems.
fact_1: It followed GPT-3.5 in the GPT product line.
fact_2: OpenAI described it as a large multimodal model.
fact_3: Its text capability was released in ChatGPT and the API.

title: Gemini 2.5 introduced reasoning-focused models
summary: Google introduced Gemini 2.5 in 2025 as a model family designed to reason through harder problems before responding.
fact_1: Google described Gemini 2.5 as a thinking model family.
fact_2: Gemini 2.5 Pro was the first 2.5 release highlighted by Google.
fact_3: The release focused on stronger reasoning performance.

title: Meta introduced the Llama 3 model family
summary: Meta launched Llama 3 in 2024 as a new generation of openly available large language models for developers and researchers.
fact_1: Meta described Llama 3 as its most capable openly available model family at launch.
fact_2: The models were positioned for broad developer use.
fact_3: The release expanded availability across major cloud and tooling platforms.

title: AlphaFold helped predict protein structures at scale
summary: DeepMind's AlphaFold became a landmark AI system for predicting protein structures and accelerating biology research.
fact_1: DeepMind says AlphaFold has revealed millions of protein structures.
fact_2: Its impact is strongest in biology and drug discovery research.
fact_3: AlphaFold became one of the clearest examples of AI advancing science.

title: PyTorch emerged from Torch contributors in 2016
summary: PyTorch grew out of the Torch ecosystem in 2016 and became one of the most influential deep learning frameworks in modern AI.
fact_1: Its early development was closely tied to former Torch contributors.
fact_2: PyTorch emphasized a Python-first developer experience.
fact_3: It became widely used in both research and production ML.

title: Hugging Face became a hub for open AI collaboration
summary: Hugging Face evolved into a major platform for sharing machine learning models, datasets, and AI applications.
fact_1: The platform hosts models, datasets, and Spaces.
fact_2: It is widely used by both researchers and product teams.
fact_3: Its public mission emphasizes collaborative and open machine learning.

title: The Transformer architecture changed AI in 2017
summary: The 2017 paper 'Attention Is All You Need' introduced the Transformer architecture, which became foundational to modern large language models.
fact_1: The paper proposed a model based on attention mechanisms rather than recurrence.
fact_2: Transformers became central to modern LLMs and many multimodal systems.
fact_3: The original work targeted sequence transduction tasks like translation.

title: AlexNet was a breakthrough in computer vision
summary: AlexNet helped trigger the deep learning boom in vision by dramatically outperforming earlier approaches in the ImageNet competition era.
fact_1: The system used a deep convolutional neural network.
fact_2: Its strong ImageNet performance made deep learning impossible to ignore.
fact_3: AlexNet is often cited as a major turning point in practical AI progress.

title: ChatGPT search added web-grounded answers
summary: OpenAI introduced ChatGPT search to combine conversational answers with current information from the web.
fact_1: The feature was announced in late 2024.
fact_2: OpenAI later expanded availability more broadly.
fact_3: The feature was designed to improve answers that benefit from fresh information.

title: Airbnb began with hosts renting out air mattresses
summary: Airbnb's origin story began when its founders hosted guests in their San Francisco home during a conference lodging shortage.
fact_1: Airbnb says the company was born in 2007.
fact_2: Its original idea centered on short-term stays hosted by ordinary people.
fact_3: The company later scaled into a global marketplace for stays and experiences.

title: Stripe built financial infrastructure for the internet
summary: Stripe positions itself as economic infrastructure for the internet, helping businesses accept payments and manage online revenue flows.
fact_1: Stripe serves businesses ranging from startups to public companies.
fact_2: Its mission is described as increasing the GDP of the internet.
fact_3: The company is closely associated with online payments and developer-friendly APIs.

title: Canva made design tools accessible to non-designers
summary: Canva launched in 2013 as an online design and publishing tool aimed at making design easier for everyone.
fact_1: Canva says it was launched in 2013.
fact_2: Its product emphasizes templates, collaboration, and ease of use.
fact_3: The company reports hundreds of millions of monthly active users.

title: Figma popularized browser-native collaborative design
summary: Figma became a major product design platform by emphasizing real-time collaboration and browser-based workflows.
fact_1: Figma describes itself as a collaborative design platform.
fact_2: It combines design, prototyping, and product-building workflows.
fact_3: Its collaborative approach made it popular with modern product teams.

title: Notion combined docs, notes, and databases
summary: Notion became popular by blending note-taking, knowledge management, and lightweight database workflows into one workspace.
fact_1: Notion describes itself as a single space to think, write, and plan.
fact_2: It is used for personal productivity as well as team knowledge systems.
fact_3: Its modular blocks and databases became a defining product feature.

title: Shopify became core infrastructure for online commerce
summary: Shopify grew into a major commerce platform by helping merchants sell online, in stores, and across multiple channels.
fact_1: Shopify describes itself as internet infrastructure for commerce.
fact_2: Its tools support businesses from small merchants to large brands.
fact_3: The platform is known for helping merchants launch stores quickly.

title: Y Combinator created a famous startup accelerator model
summary: Y Combinator helped standardize the modern accelerator model by funding early-stage startups in batches and culminating with Demo Day.
fact_1: YC says it began its startup funding model in 2005.
fact_2: It invests in batches of startups several times a year.
fact_3: Demo Day became one of the accelerator's signature events.

title: Slack helped normalize channel-based workplace chat
summary: Slack became a major workplace communication platform by organizing team conversations into channels and integrations.
fact_1: Slack describes itself as a platform for work.
fact_2: The product is built around channels, messaging, and integrations.
fact_3: Slack was acquired by Salesforce in 2021.

title: Rickrolling became a famous bait-and-switch meme
summary: Rickrolling became one of the internet's most recognizable prank formats by tricking people into opening Rick Astley's 'Never Gonna Give You Up.'
fact_1: The joke relies on misdirection and surprise.
fact_2: Its early popularity is strongly associated with message-board culture.
fact_3: It remains one of the most durable internet meme formats.

title: Gangnam Style became a global internet phenomenon
summary: PSY's 'Gangnam Style' became one of the defining viral hits of the 2010s and helped show the global scale of internet video culture.
fact_1: Its music video became one of YouTube's best-known milestones.
fact_2: The song's dance was widely imitated and parodied.
fact_3: It helped make K-pop visible to a much broader global audience.

title: The Ice Bucket Challenge blended memes and fundraising
summary: The ALS Ice Bucket Challenge became a major social media phenomenon by turning a viral challenge into large-scale awareness and fundraising.
fact_1: The ALS Association says the challenge raised $115 million for its mission.
fact_2: It spread through celebrity participation and nomination chains.
fact_3: It is one of the clearest examples of viral culture producing real-world impact.

title: Twitch Plays Pokémon turned chat into a controller
summary: Twitch Plays Pokémon became an internet sensation by letting thousands of viewers collectively control a live game through chat commands.
fact_1: The project centered on Pokémon Red and Blue.
fact_2: Its chaotic input system became part of the entertainment.
fact_3: It showed how livestream audiences could create emergent shared stories.

title: Wordle became a breakout daily word game
summary: Wordle exploded in popularity because of its simple daily puzzle format, easy sharing mechanic, and one-puzzle-per-day constraint.
fact_1: The game became a major online phenomenon in late 2021 and early 2022.
fact_2: The New York Times bought Wordle in early 2022.
fact_3: Its colored emoji share format became instantly recognizable online.

title: Among Us became a pandemic-era multiplayer hit
summary: Among Us turned social deduction into a viral online experience and became one of the biggest multiplayer games of the pandemic era.
fact_1: The game's premise revolves around crewmates and hidden impostors.
fact_2: Its popularity was amplified by streaming and group play.
fact_3: The concept drew inspiration from Mafia-style social deduction play.

title: Doge centered on the Shiba Inu Kabosu
summary: Doge became a major meme format built around photos of Kabosu, a Shiba Inu, plus playful broken-English captions.
fact_1: The meme is strongly associated with colorful inner-monologue text.
fact_2: Kabosu's photos became the foundation for years of remix culture.
fact_3: Doge later inspired derivative memes and even meme-themed cryptocurrency branding.

title: Distracted Boyfriend became a flexible caption template
summary: The Distracted Boyfriend meme became a durable caption format by mapping its stock-photo characters to competing ideas, loyalties, or temptations.
fact_1: It is based on a stock photo that later became widely remixed online.
fact_2: The format works because each character can be relabeled symbolically.
fact_3: It became one of the most recognizable exploitable image templates of the late 2010s.

title: ARPANET sent its first message in 1969
summary: ARPANET is one of the foundational ancestors of the modern internet and began with early networked computer nodes in the late 1960s.
fact_1: DARPA says the first computer-to-computer signal was sent on October 29, 1969.
fact_2: The earliest network began with four nodes.
fact_3: ARPANET helped establish the conceptual basis for the internet.

title: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web at CERN
summary: The World Wide Web was invented at CERN in 1989 to improve information-sharing among scientists and institutions.
fact_1: CERN identifies Tim Berners-Lee as the inventor of the web.
fact_2: The web was originally conceived as an information-sharing system.
fact_3: Its invention is distinct from the earlier creation of the internet itself.

title: CERN put key web software into the public domain in 1993
summary: A major reason the web spread so widely is that CERN released core web software into the public domain in 1993.
fact_1: CERN says this decision helped the web flourish.
fact_2: Open availability accelerated adoption beyond research institutions.
fact_3: This made the web more open than many competing proprietary systems.

title: Apollo 11 put the first humans on the Moon
summary: Apollo 11 became one of the most important technology milestones of the 20th century by landing the first humans on the Moon.
fact_1: Neil Armstrong was the mission commander.
fact_2: Buzz Aldrin also walked on the lunar surface.
fact_3: Michael Collins remained in lunar orbit during the landing.

title: The original iPhone was introduced in 2007
summary: Apple's original iPhone announcement is widely seen as a major turning point in smartphone and consumer computing history.
fact_1: Apple introduced the iPhone in January 2007.
fact_2: Apple described it as combining a phone, an iPod, and an internet communicator.
fact_3: Its multi-touch interface became a defining product feature.

title: Apple sold its millionth iPhone in just 74 days
summary: The early sales pace of the original iPhone signaled that smartphones were entering a new era of mainstream consumer demand.
fact_1: Apple announced the milestone in September 2007.
fact_2: The company said it happened 74 days after launch.
fact_3: The announcement reinforced how quickly the product caught on.

title: IBM Deep Blue defeated Garry Kasparov in 1997
summary: Deep Blue's victory over reigning world chess champion Garry Kasparov became a landmark moment in the history of AI and computing.
fact_1: IBM says Deep Blue was the first computer system to beat a reigning world chess champion in a match under standard tournament controls.
fact_2: The match took place in 1997.
fact_3: The event became a global symbol of machine capability in strategic games.

title: The first computer mouse was carved from wood
summary: The early computer mouse emerged from attempts to create better human-computer interaction methods and even began as a wooden prototype.
fact_1: DARPA notes the first mouse had one button.
fact_2: Douglas Engelbart's work helped shape modern input devices.
fact_3: The mouse became one of the most familiar symbols of personal computing.

title: ChatGPT search became available to everyone in 2025
summary: OpenAI expanded ChatGPT search in 2025 so that web-grounded search features were available to everyone in supported regions.
fact_1: OpenAI's update states the wider rollout happened on February 5, 2025.
fact_2: The feature combines conversational responses with online information.
fact_3: It made current-information use cases easier inside ChatGPT.

title: Claude 3.7 Sonnet launched as a hybrid reasoning model
summary: Anthropic introduced Claude 3.7 Sonnet in 2025 as a model that could respond quickly or spend more time on visible extended thinking.
fact_1: Anthropic called it the first hybrid reasoning model on the market.
fact_2: The release also introduced Claude Code.
fact_3: API users were given control over how long the model could think.

title: DeepSeek-R1 was released as an open reasoning model
summary: DeepSeek-R1 drew major attention in 2025 by positioning itself as an open reasoning model with strong performance and permissive licensing.
fact_1: DeepSeek announced the release in January 2025.
fact_2: The company said DeepSeek-R1 was fully open source.
fact_3: The launch also included smaller distilled models.

title: NVIDIA announced Blackwell RTX PRO systems in 2025
summary: NVIDIA expanded the Blackwell era in 2025 by bringing RTX PRO Blackwell products to workstations and servers for professional AI and graphics workloads.
fact_1: The announcement was made during GTC 2025.
fact_2: NVIDIA framed the products for designers, developers, and data scientists.
fact_3: The release emphasized agentic AI and professional visualization workflows.

title: Gemini 3 was released in late 2025
summary: Google released Gemini 3 in late 2025 as a major update to its flagship model family, emphasizing stronger multimodal and agentic capability.
fact_1: Google described Gemini 3 as its most intelligent model at launch.
fact_2: The release was framed as a major step forward in the Gemini era.
fact_3: Google emphasized its ability to help users build, learn, and plan.

title: Gemini 3 Flash focused on speed plus frontier ability
summary: Google followed Gemini 3 with Gemini 3 Flash, a faster model variant aimed at combining strong intelligence with lower latency.
fact_1: Google announced Gemini 3 Flash in December 2025.
fact_2: The product was positioned around speed and practical usefulness.
fact_3: It was described as helping users learn, build, and plan faster.
